---
title: "Assignment-7"
author: "Tushar Sharma"
format: pdf
editor: visual
---

# Logistic Regression for Binary Classification

## Objective

1.  Understand logistic regression for classification problems.

2.  Build a logistic regression model in R.

3.  Predict probabilities and classes.

4.  Evaluate the model using classification metrics.

# Dataset

For logistic regression we need a **binary target variable**.

We will convert the **Advertising dataset** into a classification problem.

Rule:

```         
If Sales > median(Sales) → High Sales Else → Low Sales
```

```{r}
library(caret)
library(dplyr)
```

```{r}
#Load DataSet
setwd("D:/Sem-2/R-/7,8,9,10/7")
data<-read.csv("Advertising.csv")

head(data)
```

### Creating binary target variable

```{r}
median_sales <- median(data$sales)

data$Sales_Class <- ifelse(data$sales > median_sales, 1, 0)

head(data)
```

### Convert to Factor

```{r}
data$Sales_Class <- as.factor(data$Sales_Class)
```

### Train Test Split

```{r}
set.seed(123)

train_index <- createDataPartition(data$Sales_Class,
                                   p = 0.7,
                                   list = FALSE)

train_data <- data[train_index, ]
test_data <- data[-train_index, ]

head(train_data)
head(test_data)
```

## Build Logistic Regression Model

![](images/Screenshot%202026-03-09%20143629.png)

```{r}
model_logistic <- glm(Sales_Class ~ TV + radio + newspaper,
                      data = train_data,
                      family = binomial)

summary(model_logistic)
```

### Predict Probabilities

Logistic regression outputs **probabilities**.

```{r}
probabilities <- predict(model_logistic,
                         test_data,
                         type = "response")

head(probabilities)
```

### Convert Probabilities to Classes

-   Rule:

    ```         
    Probability > 0.5 → Class 1 Else → Class 0
    ```

```{r}
predicted_class <- ifelse(probabilities > 0.5, 1, 0)

predicted_class <- as.factor(predicted_class)
```

### Confusion Matrix

```{r}
conf_matrix <- confusionMatrix(predicted_class,
                               test_data$Sales_Class)

conf_matrix
```

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

# Convert matrix to a data frame
df <- as.data.frame(conf_matrix$table)

# Plot as a heatmap
ggplot(df, aes(x = Reference, y = Prediction, fill = Freq)) +
  geom_tile() +
  geom_text(aes(label = Freq), color = "white") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "white", high = "steelblue") +
  labs(title = "Confusion Matrix Heatmap", x = "Actual Class", y = "Predicted Class") +
  theme_minimal()

```

### Accuracy

![](images/Screenshot%202026-03-09%20144044.png)

```{r}
conf_matrix$overall['Accuracy']
```

### Precisioon

![](images/Screenshot%202026-03-09%20144131.png)

```{r}
conf_matrix$byClass['Precision']
```

### Recall

![](images/Screenshot%202026-03-09%20144225.png)

```{r}
conf_matrix$byClass['Recall']
```

### F1-Score

![](images/Screenshot%202026-03-09%20144314.png)

```{r}
precision <- conf_matrix$byClass['Precision']
recall <- conf_matrix$byClass['Recall']

F1 <- 2 * (precision * recall) / (precision + recall)

F1
```

## Visualizing Class Predictions

```{r}
plot(probabilities,
     col="blue",
     main="Predicted Probabilities",
     ylab="Probability",
     xlab="Observation")
```

## **Simple and Multiple Linear Regression using Advertising Dataset**

#### Loading the libraries

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(GGally)
library(caret)
```

```{r}
#understanding data set
str(data)
```

```{r}
summary(data)
```

### Scatter Plot Matrix

```{r}
ggpairs(data)
```

### Simple Linear Regression

#### We Predict Sales using only TV Advertising.

```{r}
model_simple <- lm(sales ~ TV, data = data)

summary(model_simple)
```

### Plotting The regression line

```{r}
plot(data$TV, data$Sales,
     main = "TV Advertising vs Sales",
     xlab = "TV Budget",
     ylab = "Sales")

abline(model_simple, col="red", lwd=2)
```

### Multiple Linear Regression

Now using all advertising channels

```{r}
model_multiple <- lm(sales ~ TV + radio + newspaper, data = data)

summary(model_multiple)
```

### Prediction

-   Predict sales using the model

```{r}
new_data <- data.frame(TV=150, radio=25, newspaper=20)

predict(model_multiple, new_data)
```

### Model Evaluation

-   Shows how much variation in Sales is explained by predictors.

```{r}
summary(model_multiple)$r.squared
```

```{r}
pred <- predict(model_multiple, data)

RMSE(pred, data$sales)
```

### Residual Analysis

```{r}
par(mfrow=c(2,2))
plot(model_multiple)
```
